steps.title: Simplify and compare both sides
steps.content:
The left side is $x^2 - 97$.
The right side is $-97 + x^2$, which rearranges to $x^2 - 97$.

Both sides are algebraically identical.

steps.title: Recognize an identity and determine the number of solutions
steps.content: When an equation has the form expression = expression (the same expression on both sides), it is an identity. An identity is true for every value of the variable. Therefore, any real number substituted for $x$ satisfies the equation. That matches choice D — D is correct.
